evidence_policy: only facts directly proven by captured dumps, proxy logs, scripted read/write tests, or controlled GUI-change plus read verification

frame.length_semantics: payload byte at offset 2 contains the count of command byte plus argument bytes

checksum.algorithm: xor over all bytes from DLE STX through DLE ETX inclusive, then append 1 checksum byte

frame.frame_detection: start at DLE STX, end at DLE ETX; frame detection does not rely on a separate outer frame length

observations: loading decoderPres (user slot U01) was observed as payload 00 01 02 20 01
loading pres3 (user slot U03) was observed as payload 00 01 02 20 03
a previous preset load was observed as payload 00 01 02 20 00
after preset load, the GUI immediately re-read blocks 0x00 through 0x1C using command 0x27

input_write_observations: InA gain fader capture produced a write series with payloads such as 00 01 04 34 00 19 01 ... 00 01 04 34 00 00 00
InB gain fader capture produced a write series with payloads such as 00 01 04 34 01 11 01 ... 00 01 04 34 01 90 01
scripted read-side decode for InC used payloads 00 01 04 34 02 18 01 and 00 01 04 34 02 DC 00 and confirmed the same command/channel pattern
scripted read-side decode for InD used payloads 00 01 04 34 03 18 01 and 00 01 04 34 03 DC 00 and confirmed the same command/channel pattern
for all observed input gain captures/tests, only payload offsets 5 and 6 changed, confirming a u16le gain value field after the channel byte

input_locations.note: confirmed by scripted write 00 01 04 34 00 18 01 vs 00 01 04 34 00 DC 00 and direct diffReport on block 0x02
confirmed by scripted write 00 01 04 34 01 18 01 vs 00 01 04 34 01 DC 00 and direct diffReport on block 0x05
confirmed by scripted write 00 01 04 34 02 18 01 vs 00 01 04 34 02 DC 00 and direct diffReport on block 0x08
confirmed by scripted write 00 01 04 34 03 18 01 vs 00 01 04 34 03 DC 00 and direct diffReport on block 0x0B

input_write_observations: a passive InA mute capture observed payload 00 01 03 35 00 01 during a GUI mute toggle sequence
scripted read-side decode for InB used payloads 00 01 03 35 01 00 and 00 01 03 35 01 01 and confirmed channel byte 0x01
scripted read-side decode for InC used payloads 00 01 03 35 02 00 and 00 01 03 35 02 01 and confirmed channel byte 0x02
scripted read-side decode for InD used payloads 00 01 03 35 03 00 and 00 01 03 35 03 01 and confirmed channel byte 0x03

observations.note: confirmed by scripted write 00 01 03 35 00 00 vs 00 01 03 35 00 01 and direct diffReport on block 0x1C
confirmed by scripted write 00 01 03 35 01 00 vs 00 01 03 35 01 01; bit 0x02 toggles for InB
confirmed by scripted write 00 01 03 35 02 00 vs 00 01 03 35 02 01; bit 0x04 toggles for InC
confirmed by scripted write 00 01 03 35 03 00 vs 00 01 03 35 03 01; bit 0x08 toggles for InD

decoderPres_predicted_all_outputs.rule: all outputs Out1..Out8 share the same PEQ baseline values in decoderPres; only the output channel byte changes

observations: command 0x33 was observed during PEQ Out1 GUI changes
payload byte 4 matched Out1 as 0x04
the byte before the final reserved-looking byte changed with filter type
the 16-bit field immediately before filter_type changed together with GUI Q behavior
during controlled frequency changes on Out1/PEQ1, the second 16-bit field after channel changed while gain, Q and type stayed constant
during controlled gain changes on Out1/PEQ1, the first 16-bit field after channel changed while frequency, Q and type stayed constant
for Out1/PEQ9, payload byte 5 was observed as 0x08, matching a zero-based band index for PEQ9
for Out1/PEQ9, gain, frequency, Q and filter type reused the same field order as Out1/PEQ1
for Out8/PEQ9 Low Shelf, the payload matched the Out1/PEQ9 Low Shelf structure exactly except for the output channel byte changing from 0x04 to 0x0B
the user reported that all outputs Out1..Out8 use the same decoderPres PEQ values

band_index_u8.note: observed as 0x00 for PEQ1 and 0x08 for PEQ9 on Out1; strongly indicates zero-based PEQ band numbering

gain_or_level_u16.note: for Out1/PEQ1 Peak, this field changed from 0x0078 at 0.0 dB to 0x0082 at +1.0 dB while frequency, Q and type stayed constant; the same 0x0078 to 0x0082 pattern was observed for Out1/PEQ9

frequency_u16.note: for Out1/PEQ1 Peak, this field stepped across values such as 0x001F, 0x0020, 0x0021 ... 0x004F during controlled frequency movement while gain, Q and type stayed constant; for Out1/PEQ9 Peak it stepped from 0x011D to 0x011E during a controlled frequency step

examples.0x000A: observed when switching to shelf filters with GUI auto-Q around 0.71

q_u8_or_u16le.note: current evidence suggests the logical Q value may fit in a single byte, but the frame field is still documented conservatively as the bytes immediately before filter_type

frequency.note: observed through automated decoderPres baseline dump, known write, and before/after block diff

q.note: observed through automated decoderPres baseline dump, known write, and before/after block diff

gain.note: observed through automated decoderPres baseline dump, known write, and before/after block diff

observations: Out1 InA vs InC changed block 0x0B offset 39 from 0x01 to 0x04
Out2 InA vs InC changed block 0x0D offset 43 from 0x01 to 0x04
Out3 InA vs InC changed block 0x0F offset 47 from 0x01 to 0x04
Out4 InA vs InC changed block 0x11 offset 51 from 0x01 to 0x04
Out5 InA vs InC changed block 0x14 offset 5 from 0x01 to 0x04
Out6 InA vs InC changed block 0x16 offset 9 from 0x01 to 0x04
Out7 InA vs InC changed block 0x18 offset 13 from 0x01 to 0x04
Out8 InA vs InC changed block 0x1A offset 17 from 0x01 to 0x04
read.inference: The routing read layout is split into two output groups. Out1..Out4 use blocks 0x0B/0x0D/0x0F/0x11 with offsets 39/43/47/51. Out5..Out8 use blocks 0x14/0x16/0x18/0x1A with offsets 5/9/13/17. In both groups the stored byte equals the same input mask used on write-side.

observations: for Out1 High Pass Bypass, payloads 00 01 05 32 04 00 00 14 and 00 01 05 32 04 00 00 00 were observed
for Out1 High Pass Frequency, command 0x32 was observed repeatedly with output channel byte 0x04 and changing u16 values in bytes 5 and 6 while the last byte stayed 0x00
for Out8 High Pass Bypass, payloads 00 01 05 32 0B 00 00 12 and 00 01 05 32 0B 00 00 00 were observed
earlier crossover runs were accidentally performed on Out8; those showed the same command structure with channel byte 0x0B

frequency_u16.note: bytes 5 and 6 changed as a little-endian value during controlled HP frequency changes

observed_values.0x12: observed for Out8 HP bypass-toggled counterpart
observed_values.0x14: observed for Out1 HP bypass-toggled counterpart

mode_or_state_u8.note: current evidence suggests this byte encodes a combined HP mode/state rather than a simple boolean bypass flag

observations: for Out1 Low Pass Bypass, payloads 00 01 05 31 04 2C 01 14 and 00 01 05 31 04 2C 01 00 were observed
for Out1 Low Pass Frequency, command 0x31 was observed repeatedly with output channel byte 0x04 and changing u16 values in bytes 5 and 6 while the last byte stayed 0x00
earlier crossover runs were accidentally performed on Out8; those showed the same command structure with channel byte 0x0B

frequency_u16.note: bytes 5 and 6 changed as a little-endian value during controlled LP frequency changes

input_write_observations: a passive InA phase capture observed payload 00 01 03 36 00 01 when the GUI phase was switched to 180 degrees
scripted read-side decode for InB used payloads 00 01 03 36 01 00 and 00 01 03 36 01 01 and confirmed channel byte 0x01
scripted read-side decode for InC used payloads 00 01 03 36 02 00 and 00 01 03 36 02 01 and confirmed channel byte 0x02
scripted read-side decode for InD used payloads 00 01 03 36 03 00 and 00 01 03 36 03 01 and confirmed channel byte 0x03

input_locations.note: confirmed by scripted write 00 01 03 36 00 00 vs 00 01 03 36 00 01 and direct diffReport on block 0x03
confirmed by scripted write 00 01 03 36 01 00 vs 00 01 03 36 01 01 and direct diffReport on block 0x05
confirmed by scripted write 00 01 03 36 02 00 vs 00 01 03 36 02 01 and direct diffReport on block 0x08
confirmed by scripted write 00 01 03 36 03 00 vs 00 01 03 36 03 01 and direct diffReport on block 0x0B

observations: Out1 -> InA was observed as 00 01 03 3A 04 01
Out1 -> InB was observed as 00 01 03 3A 04 02
Out1 -> InC was observed as 00 01 03 3A 04 04
Out2 -> InA was observed as 00 01 03 3A 05 01
Out8 -> InA was observed as 00 01 03 3A 0B 01
Out8 -> InC was observed as 00 01 03 3A 0B 04
write.inference: Matrix routing write-side currently looks like a single selected input per output encoded as an input bit mask, not a freely accumulated multi-input write model. The full write map above is predicted from the observed linear output channel mapping Out1..Out8 => 0x04..0x0B and input bit mapping InA..InD => 0x01/0x02/0x04/0x08.
